title: नवदुर्गा स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नवदुर्गा स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ प्रथमा — शैलपुत्री ॥
वन्दे वाञ्छितलाभाय चन्द्रार्धकृतशेखराम्।
वृषारूढां शूलधरां शैलपुत्रीं यशस्विनीम्॥१॥

प्रथमं दुर्गा रूपं शैलपुत्री परां श्रेष्ठाम्।
पर्वतराज हिमालयजां नमामि दिव्यरूपिणीम्॥

॥ द्वितीया — ब्रह्मचारिणी ॥
दधाना करपद्माभ्यामक्षमालाकमण्डलू।
देवी प्रसीदतु मयि ब्रह्मचारिण्यनुत्तमा॥२॥

तपश्चारिणी त्वं हि तपः सिद्धिप्रदायिनी।
कठोर तपस्या द्वारा शिवं प्राप्ता सनातनम्॥

॥ तृतीया — चन्द्रघण्टा ॥
पिण्डजप्रवरारूढा चण्डकोपास्त्रकैर्युता।
प्रसादं तनुते मह्यं चन्द्रघण्टेति विश्रुता॥३॥

घण्टाध्वनिं मुखे कृत्वा दुष्टानां नाशिनी सदा।
सिंहवाहिनी दशभुजा चन्द्रघण्टां नमाम्यहम्॥

॥ चतुर्थी — कूष्माण्डा ॥
सुरासम्पूर्णकलशं रुधिराप्लुतमेव च।
दधाना हस्तपद्माभ्यां कूष्माण्डा शुभदास्तु मे॥४॥

जगत् उत्पत्तिकर्त्री त्वं ब्रह्माण्डं कूष्मभाण्डतः।
उत्पन्ना अष्टभुजा देवी कूष्माण्डां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥

॥ पञ्चमी — स्कन्दमाता ॥
सिंहासनगता नित्यं पद्माश्रितकरद्वया।
शुभदास्तु सदा देवी स्कन्दमाता यशस्विनी॥५॥

स्कन्दं (कार्तिकेयं) गोदे धारयन्ती पद्मासनस्थिता।
चतुर्भुजा शुभ्रवर्णा स्कन्दमातां नमाम्यहम्॥

॥ षष्ठी — कात्यायनी ॥
चन्द्रहासोज्ज्वलकरा शार्दूलवरवाहना।
कात्यायनी शुभं दद्याद्देवी दानवघातिनी॥६॥

महर्षि कात्यायनसुता दुर्गमासुरनाशिनी।
सिंहवाहिनी त्रिनेत्रा कात्यायनीं नमाम्यहम्॥

॥ सप्तमी — कालरात्रि ॥
एकवेणी जपाकर्णपूरा नग्ना खरास्थिता।
लम्बोष्ठी कर्णिकाकर्णी तैलाभ्यक्तशरीरिणी॥
वामपादोल्लसल्लोहलताकण्टकभूषणा।
वर्धनमूर्धध्वजा कृष्णा कालरात्रिर्भयंकरी॥७॥

॥ अष्टमी — महागौरी ॥
श्वेते वृषे समारूढा श्वेताम्बरधरा शुचिः।
महागौरी शुभं दद्यान्महादेवप्रमोददा॥८॥

शान्तिदायिनी शुभ्रवर्णा वृषभवाहना देवी।
चतुर्भुजा त्रिनेत्रा च महागौरीं नमाम्यहम्॥

॥ नवमी — सिद्धिदात्री ॥
सिद्धगन्धर्वयक्षाद्यैरसुरैरमरैरपि।
सेव्यमाना सदा भूयात् सिद्धिदा सिद्धिदायिनी॥९॥

अष्टसिद्धिप्रदायिनी कमलासनस्थिता।
चतुर्भुजा गदाचक्रशंखपद्मधरा शुभा।
सिद्धिदात्रीं नमस्तस्यै नवदुर्गासु नवमी॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
नवदुर्गाः पूजयेद्यस्तु नवरात्रेषु भक्तितः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो देवी सायुज्यमाप्नुयात्॥

एतत् स्तोत्रं पठेन्नित्यं भक्त्या युक्तो नरोत्तमः।
दुर्गा प्रसन्ना वरदा सर्वकामफलप्रदा॥

॥ इति श्री नवदुर्गा स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Navdurga Stotram ||

|| Prathama — Shailaputri ||
Vande Vanchhita-labhaya Chandrardhakrita-shekharam.
Vrisharudham Shuladharam Shailaputrim Yashasvinim.||1||

Prathamam Durga Rupam Shailaputri Param Shreshtham.
Parvata-raja Himalayajam Namami Divya-rupinim.

|| Dvitiya — Brahmacharini ||
Dadhana Kara-padmabhyam Akshamala-kamandalu.
Devi Prasidatu Mayi Brahmachariny-anuttama.||2||

Tapashcharini Tvam Hi Tapah-siddhi-pradayini.
Kathora Tapasya Dvara Shivam Prapta Sanatanam.

|| Tritiya — Chandraghanta ||
Pindaja-pravararudha Chandakopa-astra-kairyuta.
Prasadam Tanute Mahyam Chandraghanteti Vishruta.||3||

Ghanta-dhvanim Mukhe Kritva Dushtanam Nashini Sada.
Simha-vahini Dasha-bhuja Chandraghantam Namamyaham.

|| Chaturthi — Kushmanda ||
Surasa-sampurna-kalasham Rudhira-aplutam Eva Cha.
Dadhana Hasta-padmabhyam Kushmanda Shubha-dastu Me.||4||

Jagat Utpatti-kartri Tvam Brahmandam Kushmabhandatah.
Utpanna Ashta-bhuja Devi Kushmandam Pranamamyaham.

|| Panchami — Skandamata ||
Simhasana-gata Nityam Padma-ashrita-kara-dvaya.
Shubha-dastu Sada Devi Skandamata Yashasvini.||5||

Skandam (Kartikeyam) Gode Dharayanti Padmasana-sthita.
Chatur-bhuja Shubhra-varna Skandamatam Namamyaham.

|| Shashthi — Katyayani ||
Chandra-haso-jjvala-kara Shardula-vara-vahana.
Katyayani Shubham Dadyad-devi Danava-ghatini.||6||

Maharshi Katyayana-suta Durgama-asura-nashini.
Simha-vahini Trinetra Katyayanim Namamyaham.

|| Saptami — Kalaratri ||
Ekaveni Japa-karna-pura Nagna Khara-sthita.
Lamboshthi Karnika-karni Tailabhyakta-sharirini.
Vama-pado-llasallloha-lata-kantaka-bhushana.
Vardhana-murdha-dhvaja Krishna Kalaratrir-bhayankari.||7||

|| Ashtami — Mahagauri ||
Shvete Vrishe Samarudha Shvetambara-dhara Shuchih.
Mahagauri Shubham Dadyan-mahadeva-pramodada.||8||

Shanti-dayini Shubhra-varna Vrishabha-vahana Devi.
Chatur-bhuja Trinetra Cha Mahagaurim Namamyaham.

|| Navami — Siddhidatri ||
Siddha-gandharva-yaksha-adyair-asurair-amarairapi.
Sevyamana Sada Bhuyat Siddhida Siddhidayini.||9||

Ashta-siddhi-pradayini Kamalasana-sthita.
Chatur-bhuja Gada-chakra-shankha-padma-dhara Shubha.
Siddhidatrim Namastasyai Navadurgasu Navami.

|| Phalashruti ||
Navadurgah Pujayed-yastu Navaratreshu Bhaktitah.
Sarva-papa-vinirmukto Devi Sayujyam-apnuyat.

Etat Stotram Pathen-nityam Bhaktya Yukto Narottamah.
Durga Prasanna Varada Sarva-kama-phala-prada.

|| Iti Shri Navdurga Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
१. शैलपुत्री — पर्वतराज हिमालय की पुत्री, वृषभ (बैल) पर सवार, त्रिशूल धारिणी। नवरात्रि के प्रथम दिन इनकी पूजा होती है।

२. ब्रह्मचारिणी — हाथ में रुद्राक्ष माला और कमण्डल धारण करने वाली। कठोर तपस्या द्वारा शिव को प्राप्त करने वाली। द्वितीय दिन पूजित।

३. चन्द्रघण्टा — मस्तक पर अर्धचन्द्र के आकार की घण्टा धारण करने वाली। दस भुजाओं वाली, सिंह वाहिनी। दुष्टों का नाश करने वाली। तृतीय दिन पूजित।

४. कूष्माण्डा — अपनी मन्द हँसी से ब्रह्माण्ड को उत्पन्न करने वाली। अष्टभुजा देवी। सूर्यमण्डल में निवास करने वाली। चतुर्थ दिन पूजित।

५. स्कन्दमाता — कार्तिकेय (स्कन्द) की माता। कमल पर विराजमान, गोद में स्कन्द को धारण करने वाली। पञ्चम दिन पूजित।

६. कात्यायनी — महर्षि कात्यायन की पुत्री। सिंह वाहिनी, त्रिनेत्रा, दानवों का संहार करने वाली। षष्ठ दिन पूजित।

७. कालरात्रि — भयंकर रूप वाली, काल का नाश करने वाली। अन्धकार के समान श्याम वर्ण। गधे पर सवार। सप्तम दिन पूजित।

८. महागौरी — अत्यन्त गौर वर्ण, श्वेत वस्त्रधारिणी। वृषभ वाहना, शान्तिदायिनी। अष्टम दिन पूजित।

९. सिद्धिदात्री — अष्ट सिद्धियाँ प्रदान करने वाली। कमल पर विराजमान। सिद्ध, गन्धर्व, यक्ष, असुर और देवता सब सेवा करते हैं। नवम दिन पूजित।

जो भक्तिपूर्वक नवरात्रि में नवदुर्गा की पूजा करता है, वह सर्वपापमुक्त होकर देवी की सायुज्य (मिलन) प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Complete invocation of all nine forms of Goddess Durga worshipped during Navratri
Each form blesses a specific chakra and aspect of the devotee's life
Shailaputri grants stability, Brahmacharini grants tapas-siddhi
Chandraghanta destroys evil, Kushmanda creates cosmic energy
Skandamata blesses with a child's protection, Katyayani destroys demons
Kalaratri removes all fears, Mahagauri purifies, Siddhidatri grants all siddhis
Daily recitation during Navratri fulfils all desires and grants liberation
description: The Navdurga Stotram, known in Sanskrit as नवदुर्गा स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Goddess Durga. Attributed to Devi Bhagavata Purana and Traditional Navratri Texts, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to shailaputri grants stability, Brahmacharini grants tapas-siddhi and skandamata blesses with a child's protection, Katyayani destroys demons. It is also said to complete invocation of all nine forms of Goddess Durga worshipped during Navratri. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri for enhanced blessings. With 9 verses and a reading time of about 10 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Navdurga Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Navdurga Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer to nine forms of Durga - Shailaputri to Siddhidatri for Navratri worship.
source: Devi Bhagavata Purana and Traditional Navratri Texts
relatedSearches: Navdurga stotram lyrics Sanskrit nine forms
Navratri nine days Devi worship sequence
Navdurga beej mantras one for each day

seoFaqs.question: Which nine goddesses does the Navadurga Stotram address?
seoFaqs.answer: The Navadurga Stotram addresses, in fixed sequence, Shailaputri (daughter of the mountain), Brahmacharini (the ascetic), Chandraghanta (bell-crescent warrior), Kushmanda (cosmic egg-bearer), Skandamata (mother of Kartikeya), Katyayani (born of sage Katyayana), Kalaratri (dark night), Mahagauri (radiant fair one) and Siddhidatri (granter of siddhis). Drik Panchang's stotram page and Hinduism Outlook's commentary both reproduce this canonical order. The hymn devotes a single shloka to each form, encoding her mount, weapons or attributes and the specific blessing she grants. Reciting all nine in sequence is what makes the stotram particularly suited to Navratri, where one verse becomes the focal sankalpa for each of the nine nights of worship.

seoFaqs.question: When during Navratri is the Navadurga Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Navadurga Stotram can be recited as a single block once a day throughout Navratri, or its individual verses can be foregrounded one per day matching the day's presiding goddess. Drikpanchang's daily Navratri pages assign each form to its specific tithi and recommend chanting the corresponding verse during that day's puja, while many Devi mandirs chant the full nine-verse stotram at the morning and evening aarti every day of the festival. Practitioners undertaking the full vrata typically recite the complete stotram three or nine times each morning after kalasha sthapana, treating it as the day's kavacha. Outside Navratri, the stotram is also chanted on Tuesdays, Fridays and on the Ashtami of every lunar month.

seoFaqs.question: Is the Navadurga Stotram a single composed hymn or a compilation of verses?
seoFaqs.answer: The Navadurga Stotram circulating today is best described as a curated compilation rather than a single authored hymn. As both Drikpanchang and Poojn.in note, the verses are drawn from older Devi sources including the Devi Bhagavata Purana and the Markandeya Purana's Devi Mahatmya, where the nine forms are referenced in various stuti passages. Pandits stitched these into a single nine-verse stotram in standard order so that the goddess of each Navratri night could be addressed by name in one continuous recitation. This editorial origin is why printed editions sometimes show small variants in adjective and metre across publishers, even though the names and sequence of the nine forms are stable.

seoFaqs.question: What blessings does each verse of the Navadurga Stotram emphasise?
seoFaqs.answer: Each verse emphasises the specific blessing of the form it praises. As summarised by Hindu Mirror and Drikpanchang, Shailaputri's verse asks for stability and rooted strength, Brahmacharini's for tapas and resolve, Chandraghanta's for courage and removal of fear, Kushmanda's for vitality and prosperity, Skandamata's for protection of children and family, Katyayani's for victory over inner and outer enemies, Kalaratri's for destruction of negativity, Mahagauri's for purification and forgiveness of past faults, and Siddhidatri's for siddhis and fulfilment. Reciting the full stotram therefore touches the complete spectrum of Navratri sankalpa material, which is why it is preferred over chanting only one form's hymn during the festival.

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between the Navadurga Stotram and the Durga Saptashati?
seoFaqs.answer: The Navadurga Stotram is a short nine-verse hymn that names and praises the nine Navratri forms of Durga, while the Durga Saptashati (also called Devi Mahatmya or Chandi Path) is a 700-verse text from the Markandeya Purana narrating the goddess's slaying of Madhu-Kaitabha, Mahishasura and Shumbha-Nishumbha. The Saptashati requires roughly two to three hours to recite in full and traditionally needs a guided sankalpa with the Kavacha, Argala and Keelaka prefixes; the Navadurga Stotram, by contrast, takes only a few minutes and is designed for daily Navratri use by householders. Many devotees combine both: the long Saptashati on key nights such as Ashtami, and the short Navadurga Stotram every morning and evening of the festival.

seoFaqs.question: Can men and women of any age recite the Navadurga Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. As Poojn.in, Hindu Mirror and Bhakti Nidhi all note, the Navadurga Stotram is universally recommended for householders, students, women and elderly devotees, with no restriction by caste, gender or initiation. Because the hymn is praise-only and does not activate Tantric bija mantras, it is considered safe even for those without a guru. Traditional advice is to bathe and wear clean clothes, sit facing east or north, light a ghee lamp, and chant unhurriedly with attention to the names of the nine forms. Pregnant women, the unwell and menstruating women may chant mentally if they prefer not to chant aloud during specific days, with the same spiritual benefit attributed to the recitation.
